codeReview.summary: The COA module implementation is comprehensive and covers all required features: CRUD operations, Tipe dropdown (Aktiva/Passiva/Modal/Pendapatan/Beban), parent COA hierarchy, search with 300ms debounce, filter by Tipe and Status Aktif, pagination with configurable page sizes, cascade delete warning for linked Aspek Kerja, and Ctrl+A/Ctrl+C copy functionality. The implementation follows existing project patterns and passes typecheck and build. One blocking issue was found with import batch parent hierarchy handling.

issues.description: Import batch does not correctly establish parent-child relationships when both parent and child are imported in the same batch. The `parentIdMap` is built from existing database records before any new records are inserted, so a child COA with `parent_kode` pointing to another COA in the same import batch will get `parent_id = null`. Fix: Either insert records in dependency order (parents first), or build parentIdMap incrementally as records are inserted, or require parents to already exist in database before import.

Circular reference prevention in UI is incomplete. When editing a COA, the parent dropdown filters out the current COA but does not filter out its descendants. A user could select a grand-parent as a parent and create an indirect circular reference. The backend `updateCOA` catches direct self-reference (`parentId === id`) but not indirect circular references through the hierarchy. Suggestion: Add a recursive check to filter out all descendants of the current COA from the parent dropdown, or document that the backend will reject such cases.

The `deleteCOA` function returns a message about children count ('COA ini memiliki N child COA...') but this error path is not specifically handled in the UI. The COAListPage only shows `deleteWarning` for aspek kerja counts, and `deleteError` for generic errors. Users deleting a COA with children will see a generic error message instead of a specific 'has children' warning with actionable options.

Sample data in `createSampleData()` does not validate tipe values against `TIPE_OPTIONS`. While the hardcoded sample data happens to use valid values, this is a maintenance hazard. If someone adds an invalid tipe to sample data, it will be accepted silently.

The `deleteCOAChildren` recursive function does not call `saveDatabase()` after each deletion. If there are deep hierarchies and an error occurs mid-deletion, the database could be left in an inconsistent state. Consider calling `saveDatabase()` after the recursive deletions or wrapping in a transaction.

Pre-existing lint errors: ESLint is not configured for browser globals (window, console, navigator, process). This affects all renderer files equally, not specific to COA implementation. The COA code follows the same patterns as existing code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill specifies TDD procedure ('Write Tests First'), but the handoff shows 'tests: {added: [], updated: []}'. This is consistent across all foundation features (electron-setup, user-management-app, master-data-coa, etc.). Either tests are not being written for these features, or the skill procedure is not being followed strictly. The skill may need clarification on whether TDD is required for UI-heavy features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows no tests added. Skill SKILL.md Section 2 'TDD Phase' specifies creating test files first.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered and resolved an issue with renderer type declarations for preload APIs. The preload.ts exports types in a `declare global { interface Window { ... } }` block, but the renderer uses vite-env.d.ts which also declares Window. The solution was to update vite-env.d.ts to include COA APIs alongside the existing Window interface. This pattern of exposing APIs through preload and declaring types in vite-env.d.ts could be documented more explicitly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows multiple iterations fixing typecheck errors before the COA types were properly visible to the renderer. Final fix was updating src/renderer/vite-env.d.ts.

summary: The master-data-coa feature implementation is complete and functional with all required features implemented: CRUD, Tipe dropdown, parent hierarchy, search/filter with debounce, pagination, cascade delete warning, and copyable tables. The code follows existing project patterns and passes typecheck and build. One blocking bug was found in the import batch functionality where parent-child relationships are not correctly established when both parent and child are imported in the same batch. Additionally, the UI's circular reference prevention is incomplete (only checks direct self-reference). These are addressable issues but the feature is fundamentally sound for user testing.
